did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Correspondence, research, articles, course material and lectures pertaining to Dr. Smith's study of Japan.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Anthropology and Asian Studies, Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes articles and information about Japanese emperors, ancestor worship, the Japanese in Brazil, historical demography and material concerning the student protests at Cornell in 1969, correspondence with David Nathan Meyerson, and scrapbooks with newspaper clippings from World War I and World War II, material concerning Smith's participation in the Army Specialized Training Reserve Program and programs from various plays from the 1940s.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Arranged by subject.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p:
Series I. Correspondence
Boxes 1-8Box 43 Folders 11, 13-19

Series II. Historical Demography (includes Data Card Sets)
Boxes 9-11

Series III. Course Material
Box 12Box 22 Folders 3-6

Series IV. Committee Files, Awards
Box 13

Series V. Japanese Emperors
Box 14

Series VI. Morgan Lectures
Box 15

Series VII. Japanese in Brazil
Boxes 16-17Box 43 Folder 9

Series VIII. Ancestor Worship
Boxes 18-19Box 25 Folders 1-12

Series IX. Cornell 1969
Box 20

Series X. Reprints
Box 21

Series XI. Clippings -- Japan
Boxes 23-24Box 26 Folders 1-16Box 43 Folder 8

Series XII. Subject Files
Box 22 Folders 1-2, 7-16 Box 36 Folders 1-4

Series XIII. Calendars
Box 25

Series XIV. Book - Kurusu
Boxes 27-29Box 36 Folders 18-19

Series XV. Scrapbooks
Boxes 30-32

Series XVI. Newspapers
Boxes 33-35

Series XVII. Army Specialized Training Reserve Program (ASTRP)
Box 37Box 45

This material covers the years from Robert Smith's enlistment in the US Army Reserve in July 1944 to his service in the occupation of Japan in 1946. In the Army Specialized Training Reserve Program (ASTRP) and Army Specialized Training Program (ASTP) at Carnegie Tech, University of Minnesota and Yale University

Series XVIII. David Meyerson Material
Box 36

Series XIX. The W.W. Curtis Ainu Collection
Box 43 Folders 1-7

Series XX. Other Material
Boxes 37-38Box 44

Series XXI. Suye-Mura Material
Boxes 46-47

Series VII. The Japanese in Brazil

scopecontent.p: Bibliographic cards tied up in bundles.

did.unittitle: Visit of the Crown Prince and Princess,
Ancestor Worship in Contemporary Japan, First Draft
Ancestor Worship In Contemporary Japan, As Submitted to Press
Japanese Religious Life, - by Edward Putzar - text and slides,

scopecontent.p: From various shows such as: Fantasia, Hamlet, Barnum and Bailey Circus, Macbeth, Porgy and Bess, Harvey, The North Star and Ice-Capades of 1944

did.unittitle: Copies of Mainichi and the Nippon Times concerning WW II,
Copies of Mainichi and the Nippon Times concerning WW II,
Copies of Mainichi and the Nippon Times concerning WW II,
Copies of Mainichi and the Nippon Times concerning WW II,
Series XVII. Army Specialized Training Reserve Program (ASTRP)

scopecontent.p: These materials cover the years from Robert Smith's enlistment in the US Army Reserve in July 1944 to his service in the occupation of Japan in 1946. In the Army Specialized Training Reserve Program (ASTRP) and Army Specialized Training Program (ASTP) at Carnegie Tech, University of Minnesota and Yale University.

did.unittitle: Prehistory and photograph of Language and Area Program at the University of Minnesota, October 1944 ,and color photo of the survivors and spouses at the same location,
Oral history of the ASTRP Japanese Language Class of 1944, 17 to 70- by James R. Price,
Occupation of Japan - 25th Infantry Division - G-2 reports,
Newsletter published by William Bridges - Gaya-gaya is the Japanese for "making a racket",
Personal/professional papers: miscellaneous posters, correspondence, manuscripts (finished and draft)

list.item: Seventeen to Seventy: An Oral History of the ASTRP Japanese Class of 1944 - set of four audiotapes made in 1997 by James R. Price
Vol. 1 (2 copies; original and revision): (Chapter I: Entry into the ASTRP Program, Chapter II: From Slide Rule to Green Tea, Chapter III: Minnesota Experience)
Vol. 2: (Chapter IV: On to Basic Training, Chapter V: The Grand Finale at Yale, Chapter VI: In the Army (Japan and Elsewhere Parts 1 and 2))

did.unittitle: Series XVIII. David Nathan Meyerson Material
Publications - short story, screenplay, translation from the Japanese
Series XIX. The W.W. Curtis Ainu Collection
Background and Notes on William Willis Curtis in Japan,
Cataloging the Curtis Ainu Collection at Cornell,
Curtis Ainu Collection: photographs of the 50 items
Curtis Ainu Collection: Exhibit at Hokkaido Kaitaku Kinenkan,
Curtis Ainu Collection: Complete set of photographs and Certificate acknowledging donation by RJS from the Director of the Hokkaido Kaitaku Kinenkan
Ainu Project of the National Museum of Natural History (Smithsonian Institution) and Nagoya University
Maps of Brazil from research project - in two large tubes,
Maps of Brazil from research project - in two large tubes,
Maps of Brazil from research project - in two large tubes,
Order of the Rising Sun materials I
Order of the Rising Sun materials II
Order of the Rising Sun materials III
Wiswell/Smith Correspondence & Ella L. Wiswell Obituary

The following folders contain Ella L. Wiswell's correspondence with a variety of individuals and institutions. Most of the correspondence concerns the 1935-1936 study of the Japanese village of Suye Mura conducted with her first husband John Fee Embree and her subsequent visits there in 1951 (following his death in 1950) and 1968 and 1985 with her second husband Frederic Wiswell.
Reviews and Correspondence re: The Women of Suye Mura
Kawamura Nozomu- Correspondence (Translator of The Women of Suye Mura)
Ushijima Morimitsu-Correspondence (Long time student of Suye Mura in the postwar period)
Nishino Keisuke- Correspondence (Resident of Suye Mura and sponsor of 1985 celebration in Suye Mura. See much additional material.)
Shirahama Tamotsu- Correspondence (Resident of Suye Mura.)
Rudolf Janssen- Correspondence re: his paper on Occupation of Japan.
Bulletin of Concerned Asia Scholars-Correspondence re: offensive issue.
Miscellaneous items concerning John Fee Embree following his death in 1950.
First postwar visit to Suye Mura by Ella Lury Embree
Second postwar visit to Suye Mura by Ella Lury and Fredric Wiswell
Two Japanese Editions of Suye Mura: A Japanese Village by John F. Embree
Ella Wiswell-Miscellaneous lectures on Japanese Women
50th Anniversary visit to Suye Mura by Ella Lury and Frederic Wiswell #1
50th Anniversary visit to Suye Mura by Ella Lury and Frederic Wiswell #2
50th Anniversary visit to Suye Mura by Ella Lury and Frederic Wiswell #3
Brochures- Suye Mura, Hitoyoshi Town, Kumamoto Prefecture
Photograph album pages of Wiswells visit to Suye Mura
Wiswell/Smith Interview by David Plath, Honolulu, Hawaii I&II

unittitle.emph: Hajiai to yasuragi no sato- Suye mura - Heisei 13- Heisei 15- Omoide no Kiseki

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Robert J. Smith Papers, #14-25-3175. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
